name: payment_routing
description: a payment routing world across rails by cost and speed; actions route, retry, reverse

card.description: a payment routing world across rails by cost and speed; actions route, retry, reverse

rules: Each rail trades cost against speed: cheap rails (ach) are slow with lower limits; fast rails (rtp, wire) cost more.
route(rail): attempt the payment on the named rail (default 'ach') when pending or failed; it charges that rail's fee and one attempt.
An attempt settles iff amount <= the rail's limit; otherwise it fails (the fee is still spent).
retry(rail): only valid after a failed attempt; re-routes on the given rail (default: the last rail used), charging its fee again.
reverse: only a settled payment can be reversed; it claws back the delivered amount and charges a reversal fee equal to the rail's fee. Routing fees already paid are not refunded.
'settled' and 'reversed' are terminal: routing or reversing from them is a no-op.

emit.report: payment {status} via {rail}: delivered {settled_amount}, cost {fees_paid}, latency {latency}h over {attempts} attempt(s)
